title: Campaign human-gate decision
description: The file a human authors to resolve ONE campaign pause on `run_loop.py --campaign ... --resume --decision <file>` (the file-based decision_resolver). It is IDENTITY-BOUND to the EXACT paused unit: the runner honors it ONLY when campaign_id + pause_reason + checkpoint(basename) AND — when the pause is on a unit — the live paused unit's milestone_id + subsprint_id all match (read from campaign-state.json); a checkpoint-bearing pause whose unit cannot be resolved is REFUSED (never bound loosely). Fail-closed: a decision from a different campaign/milestone/sub-sprint/gate (even one whose checkpoint basename collides) is refused, never replayed. The payload is GATE-SPECIFIC: the acceptance_fix_required gate (Constitution §3.5) takes `confirm` (yes|no) + optional `route` and forbids `choice`; every other gate takes `choice` and forbids `confirm`/`route`; campaign_budget_exhausted's `choice` is restricted to raise_cap|abort (so an ambiguous decision re-pauses, never silently aborts). Gates resolved by editing the PLAN (campaign_plan_signoff -> signed_by_human:true; milestone_decompose_required -> fill subsprint_sequence; deliver_followup_required -> Deliver inserts the sub-sprint) do NOT use this file. process/campaign-loop.md §4/§6.

campaign_id.description: MUST equal the live campaign-state campaign_id (and the plan's).

milestone_id.description: MUST equal the live PAUSED unit's milestone id (printed by the CLI). Identity binding: a decision authored for another milestone — even one with the same pause_reason and checkpoint basename (e.g. a repeated sub-sprint id) — is refused. Omit ONLY for a checkpoint-less pause with no unit (e.g. campaign_budget_exhausted).

subsprint_id.description: MUST equal the live PAUSED unit's sub-sprint id (printed by the CLI). Same binding as milestone_id.

pause_reason.description: MUST equal the current campaign-state pause_reason (printed by the CLI), e.g. advisory_acceptance_pass_signoff, acceptance_fix_required, gate_hard_fail, campaign_budget_exhausted.

checkpoint.description: The BASENAME of the paused checkpoint file (campaign-state pause_checkpoint, printed by the CLI), or null when the pause carries no checkpoint (e.g. campaign_budget_exhausted). Compared EXACTLY to the live pause_checkpoint basename; an empty string is rejected (not a valid checkpoint filename).

choice.description: The selected option for a dispatch-table gate (campaign.interpret_dispatch), e.g. ship|reject (advisory_acceptance_pass_signoff), raise_cap|abort (campaign_budget_exhausted), accept_and_advance|open_followup_subsprint|abort (review_out_of_scope). Required for every gate EXCEPT acceptance_fix_required; an unrecognized (gate, choice) fail-closes to a Deliver follow-up, never an auto-advance.

confirm.description: ONLY for the acceptance_fix_required gate (Constitution §3.5): `no` ships the advisory pass (assume residual risk -> advance the milestone); `yes` (+ route) routes a Deliver fix follow-up.

route.description: Optional route for a confirm:yes acceptance_fix_required decision (e.g. deliver_fix_iteration), per the charter's tooling.acceptance.on_fix_required.route_options.

note.description: Optional free-text rationale recorded with the decision (audit only).

allOf.description: Gate-specific payload: acceptance_fix_required uses confirm (+route), forbids choice; every other gate uses choice, forbids confirm/route.
campaign_budget_exhausted accepts ONLY raise_cap|abort, so an ambiguous decision is schema-rejected (re-pauses) rather than silently aborting.
